mechanism_narrative: Extremely low FEDFUNDS (near zero) indicates aggressive monetary easing, which eventually supports asset prices by lowering the discount rate and encouraging risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve's aggressive rate cuts in late 2008 and early 2009
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is consistently near zero for sustained periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in the rate of increase of the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from a high level signals that the labor market is stabilizing, reducing recessionary fears and supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change falls below 0.2 percentage points after rising above 7%
evidence_refs: fact:Historical tendency for markets to rally before the official end of a recession
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE's rate of increase significantly slows during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous stabilization or slight rebound in both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) after a sharp decline indicates a nascent recovery in both supply and demand, which is highly bullish for corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both show positive month-over-month growth for two consecutive months after a period of decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Economic activity indicators typically bottom out before equity markets fully recover
failure_condition: SPX fails to show positive 60d returns when both INDPRO and RSAFS exhibit a synchronized rebound from recessionary lows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of severe economic contraction, a bottoming out and subsequent increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals a crucial turning point for consumer confidence and investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST shows a month-over-month increase of at least 5% after falling below 600,000 units (annualized).
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector often leads broader economic recoveries
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite a rebound in HOUST during a severe recession.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment (UNRATE) above 7% signals a weak consumer base and reduced aggregate demand, which weighs on corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:High unemployment reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where UNRATE is consistently above 7%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of very low inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 1% year-over-year, especially during a recession, signals disinflationary pressures that can lead to lower corporate pricing power and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change falls below 1% for two consecutive months.
evidence_refs: fact:Disinflationary environments can hurt corporate margins
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite low CPIAUCSL growth.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a severe contraction in both labor market health and consumer spending, signaling a deep recessionary environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both show month-over-month declines for three consecutive months.
evidence_refs: fact:Recessions are characterized by falling employment and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns when both PAYEMS and RSAFS are in sustained decline.

mechanism_narrative: During a severe economic downturn, a sharp and persistent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a collapse in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, which is highly detrimental to corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite a sharp and sustained decline in INDPRO.
